source_title: Dermal Allograft Augmentation of Rotator Cuff Repair Scientific Update
text: patients (25%) in the Regeneten group compared to 1 patient (3%) in the repair group alone ( P < .001) ■ 6 patients (19%) required reoperation, compared to 0 in the repair-alone group Takeaway: The Regeneten patch leads to a 25% rate of postoperative stiffness and a 19% rate of reoperation for stiffness when used to treat partial-thickness rotator cuff tears. References 1. Omae H, Steinmann SP, Zhao C, et al. Biomechanical effect of rotator cuff augmentation with an acellular dermal matrix graft: a cadaver study. Increased stiffness and reoperation rate in partial rotator cuff repairs treated with a bovine patch: a propensity matched trial [published online ahead of print, 2022 Aug 13].
